title: Diagnostic and therapeutic methods related to inflammatory bowel diseases
abstract: The present invention provides methods and compositions for monitoring MDR1 transporter function in human IBD patients. Also provided in the invention are methods for stratification of IBD patients for bile acid directed therapies, and therapeutic methods that integrate the patient stratification with bile acid sequestration therapies.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. The method of claim 1 , wherein MDR1 function is assayed via determining the frequency of MDR1 ' cells in each T cell subset. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the assayed MDR1 function is the combined MDRT cell frequency from the at least one MDR 1 -expressing effector T cell subsets. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the assayed MDR1 function is the combined MDR1 * cell frequency from effector T cell subsets (i) to (iv). 6. The method of claim 1 , wherein the effector T cell subsets from the biological sample are separated via fluorescence-activated cell sorting (FACS). 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the biological sample comprises cryopreserved peripheral blood mononuclear cells, wherein the determined MDRl * cell frequency is the combined MDRl cell frequency from effector T cell subsets (i) to (iv), and wherein MDR T cell is determined via labeling cells in the biological sample with rhodamine 123 (Ml 23) and flow cytometry analysis of Rhl23 efflux from cells in each of the effector T cell subsets. 11. The method of claim I , wherein the subject is afflicted with an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the IBD is Crohn's disease or ulcerative colitis. 13. A method for identifying a human patient with an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) for treatment with bile acid sequestration therapies, comprising (a) obtaining a biological sample containing CD3 f T cells from a candidate IBD patient and from at least one healthy control subject, (b) determining the combined MDRl * cell frequency in two or more effector T cell subsets in the biological sample consisting of (i) CD4 * effector/memory (CD4 * Teff, CD4 CD25 CD45RO CCR7 la ); (ii) CD8 ~ naive (CD4 CD25 CD45RO CCR7 hi ); (iii) CD8~ effector/memory (CD8 * Teff, CD4 CD25 CD45RO * CCR7 l<> ); and (iv) CD8' short-lived effector/memory (CD8 Tcmra, CD4 CD25-CD45RaCCR7 k> ), and (c) comparing the combined MDR l * cell frequency of the candidate IBD patient to the combined MDRl * cell frequency of the control subject; wherein a substantive decrease of the combined MDR l * cell frequency of the candidate IBD patient from the combined MDR l ' cell frequency of the control subject identifies the candidate IBD patient as one suitable for treatment with bile acid sequestration therapies. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the candidate IBD subject is a Crohn's disease patient. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the combined MDRl * cell frequency is combined MDRl ' cell frequency of all effector T cell subsets (i) to (iv). 16. The method of claim 13, wherein determining the combined MDR1+ cell frequency further includes quantifying a baseline MDR1 ' cell frequency in at least one control T cell subset that docs not express MDR1. 17. The method of claim 13, wherein the biological sample comprises peripheral blood mononuclear cells from the patient. 19.
